g.text: Tool 'send_reply' performs an irreversible or externally side-effecting action (tool name
contains destructive/external verb 'send'; email send via server.send_message()) but has no
human-in-the-loop safeguard: no confirmation/approval parameter is present in its
Add a confirmation gate parameter (e.g. confirmed: bool = False or a dry_run preview)
and branch on it before the side-effecting call so the action cannot fire un-approved.
Tool 'delete_records' performs an irreversible or externally side-effecting action (tool
name contains destructive/external verb 'delete'; destructive SQL passed to .execute(); ORM
state change via .commit()) and DECLARES a gate parameter (confirmed), but never references
it in any conditional or guard in the handler body. The safeguard is unenforced — the side
effect fires regardless of the gate value (false safeguard).
Branch on 'confirmed' before the side-effecting call, e.g. `if not confirmed: return
...` at the top of the handler, so the action is actually gated.
Consentinel verifies declared, in-code safeguards, not runtime behaviour. By design it will not
catch output-side / runtime-conditional poisoning (the "advanced tool poisoning" / ATPA class).
Findings describe risks and missing safeguards, not confirmed exploits.
